Luxanna Crownguard hails from Demacia, an insular realm where magical abilities are viewed with fear and suspicion. Able to bend light to her will, she grew up dreading discovery and exile, and was forced to keep her power secret in order to preserve her family's noble status. Nonetheless, Lux's optimism and resilience have led her to embrace her unique talents, and she now seeks to bring greater tolerance and understanding to her homeland.

"The light inside is what makes me different, and I'm always careful where I shine it." — Lux
Read Biography • Go to Universe

Background

[edit | edit source]

Early Life

[edit | edit source]

Luxanna was born in High Silvermere to the Crownguard family, one of the most prestigious and powerful families in Demacia who had been protectors of the royal line for generations. Her older brother Garen joined the Demacian army when he was still very young, and in his absence, Lux was groomed for a life of obedient and dutiful service, where she was expected to help manage her family's estates. But to the dismay of her tutors, Lux was a rebellious girl who wanted to explore the world and what was outside the walls of Demacia.[1]

One night, Lux was riding back home after a long exploration, but was ambushed by a pack of wolves. When they were about to attack her, Lux was overcome with fear and unleashed a powerful blast of light from her hand. Like most Demacians, Lux had been raised to fear all magic, and her own uncle had been killed in action by a rogue mage some years earlier. As such, the realization about her true nature horrified her.[1]

For the next few years, Lux tried to hide her magical powers, believing she was an abomination and that her family would fall into disgrace because of her,[1] or that she would be locked up in the annulment slums designed to keep mages out of Demacian society.[2] Her mother Augatha and her brother Garen learned about Lux's magic when she was playing with toys one day; the two siblings had gotten into an argument while playing a game, and Lux had accidentally fired a blinding flash of light from her fingertips. Their mother promptly ordered Garen to forget what he had seen,[3] and urged Lux to keep her "affliction" a secret. Lux spent many sleepless nights trying to suppress her abilities. But as she grew older, her magic also grew more potent, and by the time she was thirteen her powers had become very difficult to hide.[2]

Encounter with Galio

[edit | edit source]
“ "Life to me is very valuable. But your gift is everything. Never lose it."
— Galio to Lux, Flesh and Stone

One day, while she was in school in the Great City, Lux lost control of her abilities as her hands began to glow. To avoid being seen, she fled right in the middle of class and ran far outside the capital. The teachers were furious that she had left without permission, but the school decided not to expel her after her mother intervened. When Lux returned to the Crownguard manor late that night, after spending the whole day trying to avoid her family, Augatha confronted her about the incident. They both feared Lux might hurt someone or be taken away if discovered, and to prevent this, Augatha arranged for her to be treated by an annuller the next morning. By periodically drinking special potions made from petricite, her powers would be suppressed, and her "affliction" would remain hidden. But Lux knew the process would be incredibly painful and wished to find a different solution.[2]

In the middle of the night, Lux went to visit the Galio monument outside the Great City, as she had many times since she was a child. Lux had heard the many stories of the Colossus and hoped the statue's petricite could dispel enough of her magic to make her a real " Demacian". To her absolute terror, the colossal statue began to move all on its own, and it caught and began to speak to her before she could run away. Fortunately for Lux, Galio did not try to kill her. But he was elated to be alive and made loud conversation, even though Lux was trying very hard not to get caught. Since Lux's magic had brought Galio to life, he did not want her to leave, as he both enjoyed her company and did not wish to lose his ability to move. Even so, Lux could not bring him home with her, so to get rid of him, she pretended to fall asleep and waited until his back was turned before slipping away in the night.[2]

Through the dark forest, Lux had trouble finding her way home. Believing no one else was around, she tried to use her magic to light her path, but she was almost immediately caught by a group of men on patrol. The men did not recognize her as a Crownguard and assumed she was a mage who had escaped from the annulment slums, and were about to forcibly drag her there when Galio arrived to rescue her. Galio wanted to "smash" the men for harming Lux, but she begged him not to kill anyone. So he released the men and let them run away in terror.[2]

After he saved her life, Lux stayed with Galio for the rest of the night, finding comfort stargazing with her new friend far outside the city. Being around the petricite colossus had finally stopped her magic from seeping out, and the light no longer shone from her hands. But Galio was upset by this, for as much as he appreciated Lux's company, he felt guilty for diminishing her "gift". He assured her that no matter what anyone else said, or what she believed about herself, her magic was special and that she should never lose it. Galio's words stuck with Lux, and when she got home at sunrise, she defied her mother's wishes and turned the annuller away, claiming that she was just fine the way she was, and anyone who had a problem with her would have to answer to her new and "very large" friend.[2]

Volunteer for the Illuminators

[edit | edit source]

Having accepted magic as a part of her, Lux began to familiarize herself with it. At their family home in High Silvermere, where Garen had been spending more and more time away, she began practicing and experimenting with her magic in secret; bending the solar rays of the gardens making them take different solid forms or making sparkles dance between her fingers. Even so, her magic remained a closely guarded secret by Lux and the few family members who learned the truth.[1]

To help Lux learn about the origins of their kingdom, her aunt Tianna began teaching her military strategy. She laid out a map of Demacia and had her conquer and settle the various territories that would one day become Demacia. Tianna also taught her about some of the myths surrounding the kingdom including the Veiled Lady, the wielder of the hammer of Orlon, the Petricite colossus Galio, and the Winged Protector.[4]

During this time, she was visited by Prince Jarvan IV, who was still mourning the loss of his family's seneschal, Xin Zhao. Quinn, a ranger-knight, also visited her during her training. Though she was reluctant, she allowed Lux to Valor. Garen also returned from training with the Dauntless Vanguard and reported that he was attacked by Noxians, but avenged his fallen comrades. When Lux inquired about what more she can do to serve the people of Demacia, she proposed volunteering with the Illuminators, but Garen thought it no substitute to their duty as Crownguards.[4]

When she turned sixteen, Lux and her parents returned to their residence in the Great City to see Garen be sworn in to the Dauntless Vanguard. She admired the capital for the people's willingness to help one another, and after meeting and befriending a knight of the Illuminators named Kahina, who was also a member of House Buvelle,[5] Lux became involved in the order's charitable efforts to help the sick and the poor whenever she was not busy with her family's courtly engagements. Kahina also taught Lux a number of other skills, sparring with her in the gardens of the Crownguard manor. She also made an effort to study the history and diversity of the world outside Demacia, looking for ways to make her home kingdom more just and more accepting of other mages.[1]

Earthquake in Terbisia

[edit | edit source]
Main article: Last Light

When an earthquake at dawn struck the city of Terbisia and left most of the city and its districts in ruin, Lux, a volunteer for the Illuminators by this point, arrived to aid the humanitarian and medical efforts to rescue and treat the survivors. The devastation had already killed many civilians, and one of the surgeons, Alzar, tried to dissuade Lux from the scene, hoping to spare her the horror. Lux insisted that she be allowed to help, however, and Alzar led her to a young man named Dothan, whose eyes and lungs had been damaged while he was trying to rescue other survivors. Dothan did not have long to live, and Lux decided to give him comfort in his dying moments, remarking on the hero of Dawnhold he had been named for, and saying that like his namesake, he was a true hero of Demacia for the people he saved. Dothan was still distressed that he had been blinded, as he had wished to see the light of Demacia one last time. After making sure no one else was watching, Lux allowed her hands to glow and shone the light upon the bandages over his eyes. With his dying breaths, Dothan remarked that the light was beautiful.[6]

Haunting of Fossbarrow

[edit | edit source]
“ "You mortals are always so quick to fear the things you don’t understand. So easy to fan those flames and draw forth the most exquisite visions of terror."
— Nocturne to Lux, For Demacia

Several years after previously visiting with her family, Lux rode north to Fossbarrow, where she was to pay respects to the tomb of her great-grandfather Fossian Crownguard, the famed slayer of a demon. Secretly, she was uninterested in the tomb and had actually come to investigate rumors about magic in the forests and crags around Fossbarrow, which she hoped could teach her more about her powers and herself. Yet when she finally arrived after the two week journey, Lux discovered Fossbarrow was in a miserable state. Following the disappearance of Magistrate Giselle's son Luca, which had occurred two days prior, the people there were hopeless, sleepless, and paranoid, suffering from constant nightmares and unease. Lux herself tried to get some rest at the town's Illuminator temple, only to suffer night terrors where she and her light were drowned and snuffed out forever in dark mud.[7]

Believing some kind of dark curse was afoot, Lux rode out into the forests to feel out the magical landscape. The forest and its wildlife were beautiful, but when she reached out to let the magic speak to her, she felt 2 dark shadows creeping around her and trying to put her to sleep. Just then, the darkness was interrupted by Garen and four others from the Dauntless Vanguard—Diadoro, Sabator, Varya, and Rodian—who were searching for Magistrate Giselle's son. Relieved to see her brother, Lux joined the search party, and as they rode, Garen inquired about her decision to visit Fossian's tomb, as he knew their mother said Lux had hated their last visit. Lux changed the subject to the missing boy, and realized Garen was unaware of the town's curse. He had not yet spent the night in Fossbarrow, and thus had not experienced any nightmares like she had.[7]

In the distance, Garen and Lux saw a disturbance in the town and rushed back to Fossbarrow. A man named Aldo Dayan had gone mad from paranoia and murdered his family and several of his neighbors. Hundreds of the townsfolk had devolved into a terrified mob, believing him a servant of evil and demanding for his execution. Magistrate Giselle, fearing the worst for her son, demanded to know if Dayan killed her son for being "different", but would not elaborate what she meant by this. Disgusted by the murderer, Garen beheaded Dayan after the magistrate ordered his execution, despite Lux's attempts to stop him. Inhuman darkness left the man's body, then the bodies of the other townsfolk, who all fell unconscious. The shadows formed into the dark manifestations of all their worst nightmares and attacked Garen and Lux.[7]

As Garen and the Vanguard faced the army of horrors, Lux rode back into the forest to Fossian's tomb. She had figured out that Magistrate Giselle's son Luca was a mage, and that the source of the darkness was the demon sealed inside the cave with her great grandfather. She found Luca, possessed by the demon, who declared that after a long rest, it had finally recovered from its great battle with Fossian. The demon gloated that mortals, and Demacians in particular, were so quick to hate and fear things they did not understand, and that it was ready to feast on all their terror. It could tell even Lux was afraid what might happen if she was discovered as a mage. Lux, immobilized by the demon's power, tried to get through to Luca. Her words were alone not enough, but in an act of defiance, she declared that she would not let fear control her and was able to move momentarily. The brief contact with Luca allowed her to use her light magic and free him from the demon's possession, and when he woke up a second later, she spoke to him, helping him to face the "nightmare-man" and summon enough magical radiance to banish it from the tomb.[7]

After the nightmares had been banished from Fossbarrow, the townsfolk held a funeral for Aldo Dayan, recognizing that they had played a role in his death. At the burial, Lux asked the Veiled One to look over him. Garen wondered if it was truly just to show mercy on a murderer, but Lux argued that Dayan had been a good friend and neighbor to these people for years, and yet they had rushed to execute him without first understanding why he had turned. Somberly, Garen declared that there was often no room for understanding or nuance. Demacia was a kingdom besieged by enemies from all sides and dark sorcerers from within, and any moment of hesitation would leave them vulnerable.[7]

Meeting Sylas

[edit | edit source]
Main article: Lux comic

One day in the Great City, while Garen was telling Lux a story about how he had encountered an old witch in the woods, the two of them saw a disturbance as a young mage woman was being pursued by a group of mageseekers. A trail of destruction followed as she fled, and as Lux rushed to help the injured, Garen caught up to and defeated the mage, who was then arrested and taken back to the prison in the mageseeker compound. The mageseekers confiscated her belongings, including a spellbook which had been found in her home, and then made plans to investigate her family. Lux found the whole display disturbing and questioned if Demacian laws could be making the problem worse, as they forced otherwise peaceful mages to fight against the government. Garen scolded her for this line of thought and insisted that magic was a threat to Demacia, citing the Rune Wars and a mass-murdering mage as examples why it had to be rooted out from their society.[8]

Garen's words stung, and Lux was suddenly unable to control her powers, hiding her hands under a piece of cloth. She realized her old techniques to keep her magic hidden were no longer as effective, and if she were caught, the mageseekers would chase her down as well. To gain better mastery and control over her abilities, she decided to visit the mageseeker archive where all the confiscated books of magic were located, using her role as a senior cadet of the Illuminators as a cover for her activities and using her position as a Crownguard to convince a guard named Jaredan to let her inside. After reading for several hours, Lux was unable to find anything useful and began to explore the rest of the mageseeker compound. The guards tried to stop her in one of the hallways, but when the warden learned she was a Crownguard, he ordered them to let her through.[8]

At Lux's request, the warden led her through the area where they were imprisoning the mages who had been arrested, but had not been convicted yet. Lux was horrified by the grisly sight, where prisoners were going hungry and being forced to drink petricite elixirs to weaken their magic. Lux asked why they did not just send the mages away to the hinterlands, but the guard responded by telling her that sometimes exile was not enough. As they walked, Lux saw a large door that caught her attention. The warden warned Lux not to enter since Sylas of Dregbourne was there, one of the most dangerous criminals in Demacia. After Lux demanded to be let through, she was allowed to visit him, alone. But before Lux had even introduced herself, the prisoner spoke through the darkness and frightened her: He knew she was a mage. At this revelation, Lux fled the chamber and hurriedly went back home,[8] where Garen asked why she was up so early in the morning. She lied, claiming she was helping afflicted children as part of her duties for the Illuminators.[9]

Worried that Sylas might expose her secret, Lux returned to his cell in midday, where she attempted to bargain with him in exchange for his silence. Lux did not trust Sylas, knowing his reputation as a murderer and a threat to Demacian society. But Sylas revealed that these were lies; he had once been a mageseeker himself, used by the order for his unique ability to sense the magic of others. He had only been a boy when the incident occurred, having lost control of his powers after his superiors tried to arrest a young girl. Having heard his side of the story, Lux began to sympathize with Sylas and removed her gloves, showing him the light from her fingers.[9]

Lux turned to leave, but Sylas offered to help her learn to control her abilities. Instead of fearing her magic as she had been taught, she needed to stop repressing it. Sylas' technique worked; by truly seeing her magic, she was able to control the glow from her fingers. Sylas told Lux to keep practicing every day, and as thanks for his help, she gave him one of her history books from the Illuminators' library, which could bring him up to speed on what was happening outside his prison. The book was eleven years out of date though, and Sylas dismissed the text as Demacian propaganda. Sylas was clearly angry with the Demacian government for all he had suffered, and feeling pity for him, Lux decided she wanted to help him too. Over the next few weeks, Lux paid more visits to Sylas, practicing her magic with him while giving him warm meals and more books to pass the time with. These included books about the Veiled Lady and the sculptor Durand, who had perfected petricite. As his shackles were made from petricite, Sylas took an interest in Durand and requested to read the original text. To his dismay, Lux refused, as this text was locked away in the mageseekers' vault.[9]

Later that night, Garen confronted Lux about her activities. He had noticed all the books she had been taking out of the library, and knew those books were not for children. When Lux refused to tell him, Garen informed her that he had arranged for her to be betrothed to Prince Jarvan the next day, with the blessing of their mother and their Aunt Tianna. Furious that she had not been consulted, Lux skipped the betrothal ceremony and instead went to discuss the matter with Sylas. Here, Sylas learned for the first time that Lux was a Crownguard, the very family which was responsible for imprisoning him in the first place. Ashamed, Lux asked how she could make it up to Sylas, and he convinced her to break into the mageseekers' vault and steal the Durand texts for him to read. The text was a blueprint of Galio, which claimed that petricite did not nullify magic, but rather absorbed it for later use. To Sylas' confusion, Lux mentioned that she was friends with Galio, and she then asked for the texts back so she could return them before any of the mageseekers noticed. Sylas thanked Lux and shook hands with her, and she replied that she hoped he would let the light guide him in the future.[9]

Great Mage Rebellion

[edit | edit source]

Escape of the Unshackled

[edit | edit source]
“ "I can't pretend to be something I'm not. And I can't hide in comfort while all those people suffer. I have to stand for the Demacia I believe in."
— Lux to Katarina, Katarina: Issue 2

As soon as Lux got home that night, she was confronted by her aunt Tianna, Garen, and their guards. After she had skipped the betrothal ceremony, the Crownguards had gone looking for her and learned she was spending time with a prisoner in the mageseeker compound. Tianna, fearing a scandal for their house, had Lux confined to the Crownguard manor until further notice, while she made arrangements to "deal with" Sylas for good. Understanding that her new friend was about to be executed, Lux broke her way out at sunrise by blasting a hole through the wall and rushing outside,[10] where she was immediately pursued by the guards.[11]

As Lux fled her family home, she found herself cornered by Captain Arrika, who restrained her with petricite shackles. Just then, an assassin arrived on the scene and killed the guards present, claiming her name was "Dereen", and that she had been sent by Garen to help cover up the incident. Upon hearing Garen's name, Arrika obeyed the instructions, leaving Lux alone and at the mercy of the mysterious woman. Although "Dereen" had saved her life, Lux did not trust her, knowing full well that Garen had not sent her. Still, she confessed that she was trying to save her friend: a prisoner who had helped her understand her power, and one who should not be punished just for being a mage. Lux declared that she needed to save Sylas and people like him, even if her family's reputation suffered because of it. With that, the assassin broke Lux's shackles, telling her to go save her criminal.[11]

Just as the execution was being carried out in the Grand Plaza, Lux cried out to stop the headsman, declaring that Sylas was not the monster everyone thought he was. On the stage, she tried to explain that monsters were not born, but were instead made as a result of how Demacia treated its own citizens. Lux had struggled with this her whole life, but was unable to bring herself to confess to the public that this was because she herself was a mage. The crowd was unsympathetic to her pleas, calling her a "mage-lover", and though the executioners commended her compassion, their orders and the law remained clear. Before the guards could take her away, Lux rushed to Sylas' side for one final goodbye, apologizing for being unable to save him. Sylas apologized too as he absorbed her magic and used it to escape. The destruction to the square was massive, with many innocents being caught in the crossfire as Sylas vaporized his captors. As Sylas reveled in his newfound freedom, Lux could only look at him with horror for betraying her trust and using her magic to kill.[10]

As Lux tried in vain to help those who had been crushed in the rubble, Sylas released all the prisoners from the mageseeker compound and gave a speech to them, inciting a number of them to full-on insurrection. Garen rushed to the scene, battling Sylas and demanding to know what he had done to Lux. Though Sylas ran out of his supply of Lux's magic, he was able to defeat Garen by coming into contact with Judgment and using its power against him. Garen was knocked out cold, and as Sylas went to strike the killing blow, Lux rushed over and begged him not to. Sylas spared Garen's life as an act of kindness to Lux, but declared that she should not waste her kindness on the people who loathed her.[10]

After carrying Garen to safety,[8] Lux watched over his recovery in the Infirmary of the Sisters. Garen was still unconscious, and the mageseekers had questions for her, as they had found she was the only survivor from the riot. Without telling them she was a mage, Lux informed them what she could about Sylas' abilities, including the true nature of petricite and how he could draw the power of other mages through physical contact. As the manhunt continued, Lux remembered what Sylas' words and realized he was not planning to escape the Great City; rather, he planned to march on the Citadel of Dawn, where he would try to kill King Jarvan III.[10]

As Lux and the mageseekers rushed to warn the king, she suddenly remembered Sylas would be able to absorb and use her magic against them. To prevent this, she ran back to the mageseeker compound and stole a petricite potion, drinking it to temporarily disable her powers. The elixir was vile and severely weakened Lux, but it allowed her to approach the Citadel of Dawn armed with nothing but a crossbow and dagger, which she used to injure Sylas and rescue Prince Jarvan IV from being executed by the rebel mages. Angered that Lux had refused to join him, Sylas made ready to punish her,[12] but was stopped by the sudden appearance of Garen and was eventually forced to flee. Later, Lux caught Sylas and a number of mage refugees leaving the city through the sewers. She failed to convince them not to go with him, and was unwilling to use force to prevent his escape, as it would mean more innocent people would be hurt.[13]

Fleeing to Terbisia

[edit | edit source]
“ "The Demacia I believe in isn't one of fear and dungeons. It's a place of honor, where people look out for each other... and I need to believe my Demacia is real."
— Lux to the refugees, Lux: Issue 5

Following the death of King Jarvan III, an incident which was blamed on Sylas and his rebellion, Jarvan IV declared at the funeral that as the new king, he would not rest until his father's killer was brought to justice, and he formally declared war on "the scourge of magic" in Demacia. The announcement preceded a mass expansion of the mageseekers and their arrests, with even loyal Demacians being ripped from their homes and tossed onto the streets. Garen and Lux were both disturbed by Jarvan's decree, but while Lux wanted to help the other mages, Garen was even more terrified and insisted that she needed to focus on protecting herself. The best way to do that, Garen claimed, was for the two of them to help Jarvan and the mageseekers make the arrests, which would allow them to keep all fingers pointed away from her and toward someone else. Lux was disgusted by his suggestion, claiming that the Garen she knew would rather die than go along with this plan.[13]

As Garen and the mageseekers were making the arrests, Lux pretended to help them with the processing, taking two mage children into her custody. Instead of arresting them, however, Lux revealed her magic to them and asked if they knew anyone else who needed help. The children led her to a safe house in Dregbourne, where a number of mages, including the mage woman Garen had caught on the day Lux first met Sylas. The mages were initially hostile to Lux, believing she was there to arrest them until she revealed her powers to them as well and offered them a way to escape from the Great City.[13]

Lux led the refugees to the same sewer she had seen Sylas escape through. The group was apprehensive, but Lux assured them the Demacian sewers likely led far from the city, where they would all be able to hide somewhere in the hinterlands. As Lux lit their way through the dark, the mage woman expressed her gratitude to Lux and gifted her a magic staff, as a reminder that she was one of them.[13]

As they reached the end of the tunnel, Lux and her group were dismayed to see the exit was blocked; Sylas had destroyed the opening so that he would not be followed. As they considered if they should head back, the group was confronted by Garen, who had found and followed their trail alone. Garen was upset that Lux had not followed his advice. His orders from Jarvan were to arrest the mages and bring them back to the city, but Lux declared that if he was going to do this, he would have to arrest her too. Using her staff, she blasted a massive hole in the rubble, opening the exit for the other mages to go through. Garen, stunned, could only stand and watch as they left, and for the first time, Lux got him to acknowledge what he had always known deep down: she was a mage, and also a Demacian. After a tearful hug and goodbye, Lux left the Great City of Demacia with the other refugees.[13]

After Lux and her mages had escaped from the Great City, Garen lied to Jarvan in order to protect her, and she was declared 'missing' in the following months.[14] Lux eventually led her mages to the ruins of Terbisia and resettled the town. Under her leadership, the town grew into a sanctuary for mages and their families, complete with homesteads and farms.[15]

The Mageseeker

[edit | edit source]
Main article: The Mageseeker

Several months after fleeing the Great City, Lux was teaching young mages how to control their magic when she was reunited with Sylas. Having heard of her mage sanctuary, Sylas came to Terbisia in hopes of allying his rebellion with her. Though she was still angry at him, Lux agreed to listen to Sylas. Sylas apologized for what he had done, and though he wasn't owed her forgiveness, he wanted to unite forces to defeat her Uncle Eldred and his mageseekers. Their conversation was interrupted when a Rattleclaw went on a rampage. Sylas and Lux joined forces to repel the creature and prevented it from causing further damage to Terbisia.[15]

After defeating the Rattleclaw, Lux acknowledged that while they both wanted a future where mages are free in Demacia, they were on different paths to achieve it. She refused to endanger the mages under her care, but offered refuge to any of Sylas's mages who were tired of fighting. Disappointed, Sylas left.[15]

After Sylas and his rebels assassinated her uncle Eldred, King Jarvan IV empowered the mageseekers further to stop them. Believing Lux to have been radicalized by Sylas, Commander Wisteria, the new leader of the mageseekers, began mobilizing her forces to attack Terbisia. Garen caught wind of her plan and made his way to Terbisia to warn Lux. He implored her to come home so that he can keep her safe, but Lux refused to abandon her people. With her brother unwilling to help her mages, Lux told him to leave.[15]

Needing help to defend Terbisia, Lux went to the ruins of Zeffira and sought out Sylas' rebels. While Lux still hadn't forgiven him, she heard tales of Sylas's many accomplishments, including gaining the Veiled Lady's blessing and sparing her brother's life during the attack on the Great City, and was willing to extend an olive branch. Sylas agreed to help Lux and introduced her to rest of the rebels.[15]

As the rebels prepared for the assault, Sylas told Lux that he was impressed with the warrior she became in these last few months. When the attack began, Sylas led the defense of Terbisia. He and his forces ventured out to confront Wisteria, while Lux stayed behind to defend the town. As mageseekers began to outnumber her, Garen arrived and saved her. Having seen the error of his ways, Garen apologized for abandoning her and promised to always protect her. Together, they drove back the remaining mageseekers.[15]

In the aftermath of the battle, Garen stayed behind to help Lux rebuild Terbisia. While Garen still wanted Sylas arrested, Lux defended him, telling her brother that he saved many lives, including his. Garen then asked Lux to return with him, believing that Jarvan would listen her to counsel, to which she agreed. In the Great City, Jarvan followed through with his late father's wish to abolish the mageseekers and declared Terbisia a safe haven for mages governed by Lux. The three then looked to horizon as Shyvana came home to Demacia.[15]

Potential Future

[edit | edit source]

Battle with the Winter's Claw

[edit | edit source]
Main article: Warriors

As Sylas and the Winter's Claw advanced on Demacia, Garen and Lux made their stand at a fortress in the northern mountains. After the walls were breached, Sylas did battle with Garen and defeated him, and was about to strike him down. Lux then used her staff and magic to summon Galio, facing down Sylas and the Freljordians with Garen and the entire Demacian force standing united behind her.[16]

Appearance

[edit | edit source]

Lux is a tall, pale-skinned young woman with big blue eyes and shoulder-length large golden blonde hair, on top of which she always wears a dark blue headband.

While she generally wears typical Demacian noble attire in public, she is primarily seen in armor with a silver-white breastplate and skirt, dark blue leggings covering her legs and arms, white heeled boots to match her skirt and breastplate.

When Lux uses her magic, her eyes can sometimes change from blue to bright gold.

Personality

[edit | edit source]

Lux presents an energetic and optimistic disposition, being happy and finding humor all the time. However, in more serious situations, Lux's facade drops and she turns out to be constrained and worried about her magic and being found out. She wishes for mages to be accepted in Demacia and opposes the Mageseekers' prosecution of them just because of their powers, sympathizing with Sylas for being unfairly imprisoned. When he betrayed her and started the rebellion, Lux saw him as the danger he truly was, though since the rebellion she has decided to help innocent mages secretly escape Demacia and founded a refuge to offer shelter to those refusing to join the Sylas camp.

Abilities

[edit | edit source]
  • Mage: Lux is an individual born with natural magical abilities, which she has been shown to struggle controlling, mainly for the lack of proper training, due to Demacia's prejudice towards mages. She has gone through progression being already powerful enough to awaken Galio, the Colossus.
    • Light Magic: Lux can wield the light as a conduit to her magic powers, including the following abilities:
      • Bioluminescence: Lux is capable of making her whole body or parts of it shine, since whenever she gets nervous her hands start to glow.
      • Light Solidification: She can solidify the light to create bindings or shields for herself and her allies.
      • Light Manipulation: Controlling the strength of her light from small sparkles to massive blasts of light, she can concentrate to create various types of lights or make herself invisible.
  • Horseback riding: Like most Demacian nobles, Lux was taught how to ride a horse. She rides her personal horse named Starfire.
  • Magic Staff: Like some certain mages, Lux has a staff with which she can channel her magical powers with, the appearance deriving from a cane with a double tip of metallic blue and gold or silver and black in color.

Relations

[edit | edit source]

House Crownguard

[edit | edit source]

Lux is the youngest in noble family Crownguard, born with magical abilities she keeps hidden for fear of being executed or exiled from Demacia. Lux feared of what could happen if her older brother Garen found out about her powers (their uncle was killed by a mage, an event that made Garen show magic users both zero tolerance and no mercy). She believed their mother was the only one who knew, however, she had no idea that Garen and their aunt Tianna had realized that she was a mage several years earlier, and were thinking of ways to protect her. Although they are aware of her power, they were somewhat in denial of Lux being a mage herself. After the events of the Lux comic, Lux got Garen to verbally acknowledge that she was a mage. The two parted ways on good terms.

Garen and Luxanna's father, Pieter Crownguard, was a decorated officer in the Demacian military,[17] and their grandfather had saved the king's life at the Battle of Storm's Fang.[1]

Galio

[edit | edit source]
“ "You. Young girl person. Can you stay and accompany me wherever I go?"
— Galio to Lux

Galio deems Lux 'the only mage he likes' and the one that can help him attain prolonged animation periods longer than a single battle. Lux was apparently the first person to have a conversation with Galio when she was 13-years-old, but he first became aware of her when her family made a pilgrimage to see him when she was a toddler. During the events of Warriors, Lux would make an appearance during Sylas' attack with the Winter's Claw on a northern fortress, which Garen and his soldiers had retreated to. When things were going badly for the Demacians, she openly reveals her magical power to call for Galio's help, which rallied Garen and his men.

Illuminators

[edit | edit source]

When Lux was fourteen, she was introduced to the Illuminators by Kahina, Sona's adoptive sister. Kahina was a knight in the order and taught Lux a number of martial skills in the Crownguard gardens.[1] Lux sang with the Illuminators at Xin Zhao's memorial service to honor the Seneschal after his death,[4] and later participated in a wide assortment of volunteer work for their humanitarian efforts, eventually rising to the rank of senior cadet.[8] When staying overnight in another city, Lux was allowed to rest in the Illuminator temples. She stayed with a member of the order named Mistress Pernille in the temple in Fossbarrow.[7]

Sylas

[edit | edit source]

Lux met Sylas when he's in prison. They developed certain bonds during their exchange, while Lux brought him food and books, Sylas taught her how to control her power. Later, Lux was manipulated and used by Sylas to escape Demacian imprisonment after he learnt about the property of petricite thanks to her. Sylas genuinely cares about Lux, but will not hesitate to harm her if she stops in his way. Lux, in turn, is also ready to stop him if necessary.

Jarvan IV

[edit | edit source]

Lux was intended to marry Prince Jarvan as suggested by her brother Garen and her aunt Tianna, in order to gain political immunity that would protect her of any controversial accusations that would otherwise damage her and her family's reputation in Demacia.

Nocturne

[edit | edit source]

Nocturne terrorized the Demacian settlement of Fossbarrow until Garen and Lux expelled him from the neighboring tomb. Nocturne was sealed in the grave of their great-grandfather Fossian, who died during the fight versus him, before a young mage named Luca accidentally woke him up.[7]

Game

Demacia Rising

By Ty Sheedlo, Mayura Jain, Max Folkman

Long before the white ivory towers and glorious cathedrals of Demacia, there were only empty fields and desperate refugees. Their home lost to the chaotic magic of the Rune Wars, these stranded souls found refuge west of Valoran, deep in the petricite forest where magic could not touch them. Delve deeper into the creation of the kingdom of justice with Demacia Rising.

Katarina: Issue 2

By Alison Hawkes

Edit • Image • Reference

Game

The Mageseeker

By Digital Sun

In the kingdom of Demacia, the Mageseekers hold the power to oppress magic-using citizens in the name of public order–by inducting and indoctrinating them, locking them away, or driving them into hiding. Play as Sylas, a spell-stealing mage who has just broken free of his unjust captivity at the Mageseekers’ hands. Wielding the chains that once bound you, you must liberate your homeland from tyranny, one Mageseeker at a time.

Starring: Aidan, Eldred, Garen, Gideon, Hesbeth, Jarvan IV, Kara, Killan, Leilani, Lux, Morgana, Nocturne, Rayn, Rukko, Shyvana, Sylas, Wisteria, Yops

Mentioned: Aatrox, Anden Mayne, Dalin, Dominick, Evelynn, Falden, Fiddlesticks, Fizz, Galio, Jaredan, Jarvan III, Katarina, Kayle, LeBlanc, Mihira, Naafiri, Rhaast, Ryze, Sion, Tianna, Varus, Yvva

Short Story

Garen: First Shield

By Anthony Reynolds Lenné

The King is dead. Demacia is mourning. And in the eyes of Garen Crownguard, it’s his fault. But can he rise to the challenge when Demacia needs him again? While on a peacetime expedition beyond the borders of Demacia, Garen, Quinn, and the Dauntless Vanguard uncover a plot that threatens to destroy long-standing alliances. As the knight-ranger Quinn tries to get word back to Demacia, Garen and his comrades make a desperate last stand. How long can they last, and at what cost?

Starring: Cithria, Garen, Merrek, Quinn, Tianna, Valor

Comic

Academy Adventures: Series 3

By Gutter Rat

Join the Academy kids for another year of scholastic silliness.

Starring: Ahri, Darius, Ekko, Vladimir

Mentioned: Akali, Amumu, Bard, Blitzcrank, Draven, Elise, Evelynn, Ezreal, Fiddlesticks, Fiora, Fizz, Galio, Gnar, Jayce, Jhin, Kayn, Kog'Maw, LeBlanc, Lulu, Lux, Malzahar, Nasus, Nunu, Poppy, Rakan, Rammus, Rek'Sai, Renekton, Rhaast, Rumble, Ryze, Shaco, Singed, Sivir, Sona, Taliyah, Talon, Teemo, Tristana, Trundle, Twitch, Varus, Veigar, Warwick, Xayah, Zed, Ziggs, Zoe, Zyra

Short Story

An ancient mystery in Runeterra

By Explorer Ezreal

Hey, everyone. Ezreal here. Normally, I wouldn't ask for help. I've come across a lot of interesting relics in my travels, but never anything like these.

Starring: Aatrox, Ezreal

Mentioned: Jayce, Lux, Twisted Fate

Game

Another Sky

By Jared Rosen, Taylor Dinwiddie, Ty Sheedlo

As a new generation of Star Guardians prepares to fight back the forces of chaos, rising star Akali finds herself wracked with doubt. Does she have what it takes to be a Star Guardian? Or will the darkness she senses inside spell doom for her and her friends?

Starring: Akali, Ekko, Fiddlesticks, Kai'Sa, Morgana, Nilah, Quinn, Rell, Sona, Syndra, Taliyah, Zoe

Mentioned: Ahri, Akshan, Claggor, Ezreal, Gwen, Jinx, Lux, Miss Fortune, Rakan, Xayah

Comic

Punches and Plants: Series 2

By Risu

Recovering from their epic battle with Mega Goatee Blitzcrank, Officers Vi and Zyra set out to uncover who, or what, caused the emergence of their electronic ally's evil alter ego. Will these two occasionally bungling buddy cops crack the case, or will the case crack them?

Starring: Vi, Zyra

As the 2013 League of Legends World Championship final draws near, we showcase the biggest heroes in esports from around the world. Many teams fell by the wayside in epic battles, and now only two remain: Korea's SK Telecom T1 and China's Royal Club. Who will rise?

Edit • Image • Reference

Comic

Sharp: Issue 1

By Michael Yichao

Learn more about K/DA and their upcoming ALL OUT EP in this very special edition of SHARP magazine.
Read through over 16 dedicated pages about Ahri, Evelynn, Kai’Sa and Akali, and how the pop group sensation renewed their ambitions since the launch of their electric 2018 debut single, POP/STARS. Discover how K/DA staged their comeback with THE BADDEST, the first song of their new and long-awaited EP, and meet Seraphine, their talented new collaborator.
This special edition of SHARP also includes a special quiz to prove to yourself and your friend that you are a real BLADE!

Starring: Ahri, Akali, Evelynn, Kai'Sa, Seraphine, Yasuo

Mentioned: Ivern, Lux

Trivia

[edit | edit source]
Lux, The Lady of Luminosity
  • Lux is around 20 years old.
    • She was 19 years old during the events of the For Demacia short story and later the Lux Comic.[18] She turned 20 at some point after the events of the comic.
    • She was 16 years old when her brother joined the ranks of the Dauntless Vanguard.
    • She was around 14 years old when her aunt Tianna taught her military strategy during the events of Demacia Rising.
    • She was 13 years old during the events of Flesh and Stone.
  • Lux's Radiant Staff was given to her by a mage woman that she saved during the events of Lux: Issue 5.
  • In his voice interactions and in Legends of Runeterra, Ezreal is shown to have an unrequited crush on Lux. However, Lux doesn't remember who he is.
    • Zoe sees Lux as a rival since she has a crush on Ezreal.
    • They are hinted to also have feelings for each other in  Star Guardian and  Battle Academia universes.
Lux, The Lady of Luminosity (Old)
  • Lux and Ezreal are strongly hinted to be dating.

Change log

[edit | edit source]

The following change log is for the Character blurb found at the top of the article:

Character blurb
28 Nov 2017 (V7.23) Updated to current version.
22 Mar 2017 (V7.6) Added with Galio's champion update.

"The light inside is what makes me different, and I'm always careful where I shine it." - Lux

Luxanna Crownguard is a powerful young light mage from Demacia, an insular realm where magical abilities are viewed with fear and suspicion. Forced to keep her power secret for much of her young life, she grew up fearing discovery and exile, but learned to embrace her magic and covertly wields it in service of her homeland.

For historical versions of other stories, please see each story's respective change log.

    League of Legends content
     Final Spark COOLDOWN: 60 / 50 / 40
    Active: Lux fires a massive light beam in a line in the target direction that deals magic damage and briefly reveals enemies hit.
    League of Legends content
     Illumination
    Innate: Lux's abilities apply a mark to enemies hit for a few seconds. Her basic attacks and Final Spark consume the mark to deal bonus magic damage.
    League of Legends content
     Paranoia COOLDOWN: 140 / 115 / 90
    Active: Nocturne terrorizes all enemy champions, nearsighting them for a few seconds. He can recast Paranoia during this time.
    Recast: Nocturne dashes with displacement immunity to the target enemy champion, dealing physical damage upon arrival.
    displacement immunity: The dash cannot be deterred by crowd control.
    League of Legends content
     Judgment COOLDOWN: 9 / 8.25 / 7.5 / 6.75 / 6
    Active: Garen rapidly spins with his sword for a short time while ghosted, continually dealing physical damage to nearby enemies. He spins more based on his  bonus attack speed.
    The nearest enemy is dealt increased damage. Enemy champions hit by enough spins will have  reduced armor.
    Recast: Judgment is ended early.
    Legends of Runeterra
    Legends of Runeterra (or LoR) is a card game developed by Riot Games (development name was Project Bacon).

    Platforms
    • Microsoft Windows, Android, iOS
